{"key_event":"French Revolution panic and wartime repression","reasoning":"Britain had a comparatively capable fiscal-military state, strong navy, expanding taxation and administration, but enforcement still relied heavily on local elites and patronage rather than a fully penetrative bureaucracy. Civil society was unusually vibrant through press, clubs, dissenting networks, petitions and abolitionism, yet the unreformed franchise, aristocratic dominance, sedition prosecutions, suspension of habeas corpus, and repression of radical societies limited its ability to constrain the state.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":6.3,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":7.0}
